assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary balance that is exposed to foreign exchange movements in the consolidated financial statements.
The question is limited to hedge accounting models potentially available in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16.
The task is to identify candidate accounting treatments, not to conclude which one is permitted on the full facts.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency receivable is a natural candidate for a fair value hedge of its exchange-rate exposure.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The foreign-currency settlement of the dividend can be analysed as variability in cash flows, making cash flow hedge accounting a distinct model to consider.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 identifies a separate hedge-accounting model in consolidation when foreign-currency risk is managed as part of a net investment in a foreign operation.
